name: What are the common mistakes that kill a falling object injury claim?
description: Delaying medical treatment and failing to report every affected body part are the most common mistakes. Avoid giving recorded statements to insurance adjusters

mainEntity.name: What are the common mistakes that kill a falling object injury claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Delaying medical treatment and failing to report every affected body part are the most common mistakes. Avoid giving recorded statements to insurance adjusters without a lawyer, as they may use your words to deny or minimize your claim.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you have been struck by equipment or a falling object, the biggest mistake is failing to seek immediate medical care. Insurance adjusters will use any delay in treatment to claim that your injury happened somewhere else, like at home or over the weekend. Another common error is giving a recorded statement to the insurance adjuster before you've spoken to a lawyer.

You should also avoid failing to list every body part that hurts. If a falling object hits your shoulder but your neck starts hurting two days later, you must report the neck pain immediately so it is included in the claim.

Key factors include consistent medical documentation and avoiding early "settlement" offers that don't account for future surgeries. Many workers also mistakenly believe they must use the company doctor forever; in many states, you have the right to change doctors. An attorney ensures you don't fall into these traps that insurance companies set to minimize their payouts.
